title: Queue whispers for offline recipients
description:
What: Add offline_messages DB table + enqueue() + drain() to relay/src/store.rs so Whisper messages survive offline recipients.

NOTE: store.rs currently has NO offline queue at all (no enqueue, no drain, no table). This must be created from scratch.

Algorithm:
1. In the schema SQL in store.rs (migrate or new), add after the existing table creation:
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS offline_messages (
       id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
       to_pub_id  TEXT NOT NULL,
       payload    TEXT NOT NULL,
       created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
       expires_at TEXT NOT NULL
   );
2. Implement pub async fn enqueue(&self, to_pub_id: &str, payload_json: &str) -> Result<()>:
   - INSERT INTO offline_messages (to_pub_id, payload, expires_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, datetime('now', '+72 hours'))
   - Return Ok(()) or propagate sqlx error
3. Implement pub async fn drain(&self, pub_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>>:
   - In a single transaction:
     a. SELECT payload FROM offline_messages WHERE to_pub_id = ?1 AND expires_at > datetime('now') ORDER BY id ASC
     b. DELETE FROM offline_messages WHERE to_pub_id = ?1
   - Return the collected payloads

Defensive rules:
- expires_at stored as 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' via datetime() only — NOT RFC 3339, NOT Unix timestamp (CLAUDE.md §9)
- drain SELECT+DELETE must be in one transaction for atomicity (CLAUDE.md §10); messages are at-most-once
- All DB errors returned as Result, never unwrap() on DB ops
- drain deletes ALL rows for pub_id (including non-expired) to avoid stale entries accumulating

Tests:
- test_enqueue_drain_roundtrip: enqueue one message, drain, verify payload byte-equal; oracle = independently constructed JSON string
- test_drain_ordering: enqueue two messages for same pub_id, drain, verify both returned in insertion order
- test_drain_expiry: enqueue with past expires_at via raw SQL INSERT, drain, verify empty result
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-relay enqueue OR drain passes; all 3 unit tests listed above pass; clippy -D warnings clean on nie-relay
